title: Racosperma eremophilum
taxonomy: Racosperma eremophilum is a plant species that was previously classified within the genus Eremophila. However, due to ongoing taxonomic revisions, some sources may still refer to it under the genus Eremophila. The plant belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae, now commonly recognized as part of the family Orobanchaceae in some classifications. This species is native to Western Australia, where it grows as a shrub in various habitats. It is also known as Eremophila 'Hammondii', which reflects its classification under the Eremophila genus.

intro: Racosperma eremophilum, also known as Eremophila or Emu Bush, is a flowering shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the management of skin conditions like eczema.
